rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORA MARY SUTTON JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of An Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition for final distribution or I&A or accounting has been filed, as required by Probate Code Section 10953(b) and as directed by this Court on 12/7/21. There was no order discharging Clifton Sutton either as the executor or as the trustee of the Grandvel Andrew Jackson Irrevocable Trust B, so no order discharging bond. -- Platte River Insurance Company's Bond Upon Qualifying of Executor in the amount of $10,000 was filed on 3/29/06. -- Platte River Insurance Company's Qualifying Bond of Trustee of the Grandvel Andrew Jackson Irrevocable Trust B in the amount of $40,000 was filed on 11/18/03 and Additional Bond of Trustee in the amount of $1,210,000 was filed on 9/7/06 in case PCN-01-281686. Mr. Bennett, as executor of Clifton Sutton's will, would presumably be the person with knowledge about assets that Clifton Sutton might have transferred to himself from this estate prior to his death (without a Court order, as no distribution order has been entered in this case). Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** GUARDIANSHIP OF HAYDEN ANGELO DE BARROS CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TRANSFER to CONTRA COSTA County
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) On Schedule F - Liabilities there are 2 entries for Costco 2018 food for $8,526.00 and 2019 $6,810.00. The court requested receipts for these entries. Guardian attached Costco receipts for 2/1/2018 to 12/31/2020 to the Third Supplement. It appears during 2018 and 2019 $4,734.20 in purchases were made for gas, many $2.00 unknown entries, adult gummies, shipping tape, centrum silver men/women 50+, melatonin, wine, and vodka. It appears starting 01/03/2020 which is not covered by the entries on Schedule F - Liabilities $1,237.31 in purchases were made for tires, 50 inch tv, gas, centrum silver men 50+, and other unknown $2.00 entries. 2) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 6 states, "No other reimbursement for childcare are allowed without prior court approval. No court approval was sought for $5,385.00 in childcare expenses already reimbursed in this account. Examiner requested receipts for all entries. 3) On 04/08/2019 the court order page 2 line 9 states "Refund of $3,753.74 for life insurance premium paid shall be reflected in the next account." No such refund appears in the account. In the Supplement filed 07/21/202 page 1 lines 19-20 allege the guardian made a deposit of $3,753.53 to the Guardianship estate account which appears to be the wrong amount. 4) Examiner requested the legal basis for the guardianship paying legal expenses related to setting up the ward's inheritance estate. No legal authority provided. Examiner requested attorney timesheets for all legal expenses paid to ARCR. No timesheets provided. 5) Examiner requested legal authority for including the items listed on Schedule F Liabilities and to file receipts for all entries. No legal authority provided. No receipts provided for many entries on Schedule F Liabilities. 6) The court does not have a proposed order for the amended petition for change of venue.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if "all acts and proceedings of" the co-executors "as reflected on this Second Account and Report of Executors be confirmed and approved." 1) The disbursements schedule does not show full descriptions of each payment, such as the fiscal year or billing period covered by the many property tax payments, many of them appearing to be duplicates. Copies of property tax bills were attached to the supplement filed on 2/10/22, but the disbursements schedule still was not supplemented to show which payments are supported by those bills. After going through each bill to try to determine which disbursement corresponds to which bill, three disbursements still appear to be duplicate payments: $8,356.49 for "York Tax" on 4/3/19; $8,379.51 for "York Tax Supplemental" on 5/21/19; and $3,569.15 for "York Tax Supplemental" on 5/21/19. 2) It is also noted that at least five of the property tax payments included a 10% penalty and fees. Co-executors do not address why property taxes were paid late. There was over $1,211,000 in cash and cash equivalents and over $566,000 in mutual funds on hand as of the date of death. 3) Several documents attached to the second amended second account (rental agreements) showed the lessor as Ingeburg Maier, but the co-executors never explained this inconsistency until asked. In their supplement filed on 2/10/22, they disclose that she was Decedent's wife and that "Decedent petitioned for and received a spousal property order prior to his death, but never retitled the assets" without further detail. Fortunately, since the properties are in San Francisco, a search on the Court's register of actions turned up an estate for Ingeburg Maier von Uckermann (case PES-09-292022). It is unclear if title has been cleared on the properties. 4) Nothing has been filed yet to confirm that the bank statement attached to the second amended second account was an original. [LRSF 14.34 D. and CRC 7.575(b).] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD ATTILIO DEMARTINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition requests that cash distribution to the 9 heirs/assignors residing in Italy be made to counsel for the assignee in trust. 2) Prior to her death, Albina DeMartini, one of the heirs/assignors in Italy, agreed to assign 25% of her share to Fraser & Fraser, but the administrator of her estate objected to the 25% assignment. The administrator and Fraser & Fraser agreed to reduce the assignment share to 20%. Fraser & Fraser does not agree to reduce the 25% assignment shares of the other 9 heirs/assignors in Italy. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARION HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/29/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice has been filed. No proposed order has been submitted. As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575(a).] The conservatee's bank statement immediately preceding the appointment of the conservator(s) must be filed, not just the conservatorship estate's bank statements. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] The total income shown on Schedule 1 does not agree with the Summary of Account. There are numerous payments for "Home Supplies" and "Insu[r]ance" (presumably homeowner's?) or "Repairs" and even property tax, when there is no real property in the conservatorship estate. Reimbursements to individuals must disclose in the description of the disbursement who the original payee was. Original receipts may need to be filed. The co-conservators are reportedly also the co-trustees of the conservatee's trust, which has not been brought under Court supervision. The attorney fees are to be a lien against trust real property, but the petition caption does not disclose this and, in any event, the request for a lien against the trust must be disclosed to the trust beneficiaries. A request for the return of assets comes under Probate Code section 850, which has specific notice requirements. The signature/verification page of the copy of the petition received by the assigned Examiner is virtually illegible. After the CIU report is filed, proof of payment of the assessment fee, if ordered, must be filed before an accounting can be approved. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR LEE JUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Issues: 1) Petitioner must give 15 days' notice of the amended petition on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120. (See CRC 7.53). 2) Petitioner amended the account to end 12/31/2021. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (See LRSF 14.34 D.) 3) Please explain why the attorney is requesting $435.00 for filing an amended petition when there is no court filing fee for an amended petition. It appears the calculations for distributions needs to be amended. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARSHA FLORINE MONTGOMERY-LOCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/06/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) 15 day's notice of this petition must be re-done. Notice was given prior to the petition being filed. 2) Please explain and provide the legal basis for the dislcaimers of interest filed by Michael Matthews, Miosha Montgomery McCann,and Tasha Bell have been demmed incomplete, and are therefore ineffective, as they do not explain whether the person disclaiming the interest has any issue. 3) Please state the issue for Michael Matthews. 4) Please state the issue for Miosha Montgomery McCann. 5) Please state the issue for Tasha Bell. 6) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. (See LRSF 14.35 I. 6) a.) 7) The proposed order does not match what is requested in the petition. Please provide an update proposed order that mataches what has been requested in the petition and amendment. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 03/21/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH JESSE ARNELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The I&A filed 09/13/2021 alleges $2,781,354.40 in assets. Petition page 3 lines 9-11 alleges the I&A was filed September 22, 2021 and show assets at $2,781,271.41 and alleges the total assets are $2,781,271.41. This appears to be wrong information in the petition. 2) Please file receipts for Courier fees totaling $623.11. Please explain the legal authority for this entry and file receipt for EVP - Estate valuation for $153.60. Please file receipts for Express mail for $480.50. Please explain why the petitioner requests $450.00 for the final account petition when the court filing fee was $435.00. Please file a receipt for the $53.80 in courier fees. 3) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (See LRSF 14.34 D.) 4) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. (See LRSF 14.35 I. 6) b.) 5) Please explain if all the cash has been held in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/29/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Second Amended Petition Confirming Trust Asset was filed on 1/31/22, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. The settlor's interest in the real property at issue appears to be 50% interest in 542-546 26th Avenue, not "542 26th Avenue". Was there a condo conversion of a two-unit building? The petitioners should address this discrepancy in a SUPPLEMENT to the second amended petition. Notice of the Second Amended Petition must be mailed, along with a copy of the pleading and the supplement, at least 30 days before 3/29/22, in order to avoid another continuance. Alternatively, waivers of notice by all persons entitled to notice could be filed. If more time is needed to file the SUPPLEMENT, clarifying the settlor's interest in the property, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request a further continuance. (It is unclear why a fully amended petition was filed merely to supplement paragraph 9. Also, the purpose of the two identical supplements filed on 11/30/21 and 1/31/22 is unclear. Since a second amended petition has been filed, all prior petitions are superseded by it.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PHILLIP S. BERRY TRUST DATED APRIL 20, 2009 AS AMENDED, SUB TRUST FOR THE BENEFIT OF DAVID B
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And Appointing Nominated Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/29/22 by the court. The court is not satisfied that venue is proper in this county. Petitioner shall file the Probate Code Section 16016.7 notice that issued when Union Bank became trustee (by obtaining a copy from Union Bank if he does not have a copy available to him) or other evidence from Union Bank regarding the principal place of administration of the trust in September 2021. Also, petitioner must explain why the petition that resulted in Union Bank's appointment was litigated in Contra Costa County. Additionally, petitioner must brief why the proposed new limitations on the new trustees do not constitute amendments to the trust that must be presented in a newly noticed petition. Finally, a guardian ad litem must be appointed for Ozzy in connection with this petition before the petition can be heard because the proposed new limitations (conditions) on the new trustees may be adverse to Ozzy's interest in support. Filed stamped copy of any new pleadings filed to be delivered at least two weeks prior.

Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN LEAL FAMILY TRUST, DATED JUNE 20, 1995 as Amended and Restated thereafter
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate Section 850 Petition To Recover Funds Distributed From Trust To Beneficiaries Without First Satisfying Assignment Lien, And
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2022. Second continuance same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3. Must give 30 day's notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/09/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must file the original will or a certified copy of the will. Only a copy of the will attached to the petition is not sufficient. (Probate Code 8200 et seq.) 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF J ANTHONY WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/22/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of publication has been filed. Only one form DE-147S has been filed for one of the proposed personal representatives. Item 8 of the petition does not include the decedent's mother. Who is/was she and, if she is deceased, when did she die? [LRSF 14.18 D.] A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed by both petitioners and verified by at least one of them, must be filed to disclose information about the decedent's mother. Courtesy copies of all papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/11/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It is unclear why a Proof of Service - Civil was filed, indicating that notice of a Declaration was served on one of the petitioners[Probate Code Sect. 1201.])
